title: Trump administration pushes nations to sign ‘trade over aid’ declaration
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio called it an opportunity to use the U.N. system to “promote America First values,” according to a cable reviewed by The Post.

title: Justice Sotomayor issues rare apology for remarks about conservative colleague
description: The extraordinary public apology highlights rifts on the court dominated by a 6-3 conservative majority.

title: U.S. sends thousands more troops to Mideast as Trump seeks to squeeze Iran
description: The infusion of firepower coincides with the administration’s maritime blockade against the regime in Tehran and as mediators urge both sides to extend their ceasefire.

title: Prosecutors add terrorism charge in new D.C. pipe bomb indictment
description: Brian Cole Jr. is accused of placing explosives near the RNC and DNC headquarters the night before the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection.

title: Senate rejects resolution to block Trump from striking Iran
description: The war powers resolution was the fourth to fail in the chamber since the war’s start — but some Republicans expressed fresh concern about the war as it approaches the two-month mark.

title: White House touts refunds, as most Americans say taxes are too high
description: The president hopes voters will focus on the benefit of “No Tax on Tips” and other breaks, but economists say higher gas prices will negate most of those benefits.

title: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
description: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: What Orban's loss means for far right movements around the world
description: The defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban at the polls is fueling questions about what's in store for far-right movements in other countries that exploit extremist conspiracies to win.

title: How Swalwell rumors broke through
description: Before allegations against Eric Swalwell surfaced in the media, they gained momentum online after a network of accusers came together to share stories of alleged assault by the California Democrat.

title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
description: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

title: Trump’s reversal on day care upends a bipartisan push to lower costs
description: Lawmakers and advocates were gaining momentum until the president backtracked on his campaign promise.

title: Top CDC director pick Erica Schwartz tests White House political balancing act
description: Schwartz would be Trump’s third nominee to lead the agency. The administration is also said to be looking at three other people to serve in senior roles supporting her.

title: How to force the House to do what you want
description: The Washington Post’s essential guide to power and influence in D.C.

title: Trump adviser Sebastian Gorka seeks top counterterrorism job
description: The maneuvering comes a month after Joe Kent, the National Counterterrorism Center’s previous director, resigned in protest over the Iran war.

title: Trump falsely claimed Spanberger added taxes. Here are the facts.
description: Republicans keep criticizing Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger over proposed tax increases by Democratic legislators that never passed and that she never endorsed.

title: How Eric Swalwell rose to the top of Democratic politics as rumors followed him
description: Allies such as Rep. Nancy Pelosi and Sen. Ruben Gallego say they knew nothing about the misconduct allegations, with Gallego saying the congressman led a “double life.”

title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
description: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

title: Tony Gonzales' resignation sparks uncertainty about what comes next
description: With the resignation of former Congressman Tony Gonzales, it's unclear who will represent the Southern Texas district next or when the state's Gov. Greg Abbott will call a special election.

title: Eric Swalwell facing new sexual assault allegations after resignation from Congress
description: Eric Swalwell is facing new allegations of sexual assault, days after the California Democrat formally submitted his resignation from Congress.

title: How Congress' workplace culture made accountability elusive
description: Congress was late to adopt workplace laws that protect private sector employees. How has that shaped its culture? NPR speaks with Rutgers professor James R. Jones, author of "The Last Plantation."

title: Vance praises Trump, while subtly differentiating himself at Georgia event
description: At a gathering of conservative college students, the vice president expressed respect for Pope Leo XIV and empathized with concerns about high costs of living

title: Woman accuses Eric Swalwell of rape in 2018, reports it to authorities
description: Her attorney said at a news conference that she will not present evidence to the media.

title: DOJ moves to undo Jan. 6 rioters’ convictions for seditious conspiracy
description: President Donald Trump last year commuted the prison sentences of 12 members of the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers.

title: What Swalwell’s exit means for Democrats in California’s governor race
description: Billionaire Tom Steyer and ex-congresswoman Katie Porter stand to benefit the most in a Democratic field with no clear front-runner, several strategists said.

title: DOJ accuses Biden administration of weaponizing abortion access law
description: A long-awaited report from the Justice Department’s “Weaponization Working Group” alleges misuse of the Face Act. But the evidence is limited, and the Trump administration has itself been criticized for misusing law enforcement.

title: Appeals court again blocks contempt inquiry into deportation flights
description: Chief U.S. District Judge James E. Boasberg of D.C. has clashed with the Trump administration over dozens of Venezuelan migrants’ removals to a prison in El Salvador.

title: Trump says Iran talks may resume this week, but opposes enrichment compromise
description: The lead U.S. negotiator, Vice President JD Vance, has sought a moratorium on uranium enrichment of at least 20 years. Tehran’s offer would last up to five.

title: Former Congresswoman speaks on sexual harassment resignations in the House
description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with former Rep. Jackie Speier, who represented a California district as a Democrat, about sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill following the #MeToo movement.

title: Under pressure from President Trump, can the filibuster survive 2026?
description: President Trump wants to do away with the filibuster in order to pass the Save America Act. But many Senate Republicans are reluctant, wary of what it would mean if they were to lose their majority.

title: Justice Department says Biden DOJ weaponized law to go after anti-abortion activists
description: The Trump administration has said that enforcement of the FACE Act by the Biden DOJ represents "the prototypical example" of the weaponization of the law against conservatives.

title: Fired Fannie Mae workers can’t sue Bill Pulte for defamation, a judge rules
description: A federal judge said President Trump’s federal housing director cannot be held liable in court under a decades-old law.

title: Ex-Pence aide Olivia Troye plans to run for Congress in Virginia as a Democrat
description: She joins other former Republicans and Trump administration officials running for office as critics of the president. The district would open up if voters approve a new map.

title: Why have so few people been kicked out of Congress?
description: The Washington Post’s essential guide to power and influence in D.C.

title: What happened when Trump told a city to forget DEI or lose federal money
description: The debate in Fort Worth echoes a dilemma that local leaders are grappling with around the country: End diversity programs or risk losing all federal funding.
